meta.title: Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8 engine — AJ26 and AJ27. X308 and X350 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 15+ vetted Jaguar specialists today.

@graph.name: Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8 Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ27 engine typically costs £2,500–£5,500 supply only, with the supercharged AJ27S sitting at £3,500–£8,500. Fitting adds £900–£1,500. Main dealer new engines can exceed £25,000. Timing chain tensioner replacement alone can cost £800–£2,500 if caught early.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ26/AJ27 is rated ★★★☆☆. It's a magnificent engine when properly maintained, but it has two notorious weak points: Nikasil cylinder bore lining failure on pre-2000 engines (resolved with cast-iron liners from 1999) and plastic timing chain tensioners that break up with age. The aluminium block is also sensitive to overheating.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8?

acceptedAnswer.text: Plastic timing chain tensioner failure (the most critical — can destroy the engine), Nikasil liner failure on pre-2000 cars, water pump and cooling system issues, and overheating damage. The plastic tensioners should be replaced with metal units as a preventative measure.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and body condition. Against a well-kept X308 XJ worth £3,000–£8,000, a £3,800 recon is a significant investment — assess carefully. For an XJR with strong provenance, replacement is often worthwhile. The XJ's classic status and aluminium body mean good examples are worth saving.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 4.0 V8 uses AJ26 (pre-2000) or AJ27 (2000-2009) codes; the supercharged version is AJ27S. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the AJ27's timing chain tensioners, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Replacing the plastic tensioners with metal units (£800–£2,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain failure (£2,500–£8,500 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Make sure the plastic timing gear has been replaced with factory metal components.

eraMap.title: The XJ 4.0 V8 THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: The original 4.0 V8 — smooth and potent, but Nikasil liners (early cars) and plastic timing tensioners are the main watchpoints.
The XJR's 370 bhp supercharged version — same block with Eaton M112 blower; tensioner and cooling system risks remain.
Early X350 carried the 4.0 V8 before the 4.2; nikasil largely resolved but plastic tensioners still need addressing.

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8 Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs, warranty claims; dealer costs can exceed £25,000

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an XJ 4.0 V8 engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Timing chain tensioner replacement is a significant job but well documented.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: If caught early; upgrade to metal tensioners
Rebuild with iron liners; often cheaper to replace the engine
Preventative — replace with metal unit at 60,000 miles
If head gasket or block damage has occurred, replacement may be cheaper
Flush at 80K miles; don't trust "sealed for life"

buyingChecks.title: Buying This XJ 4.0 V8? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Jaguar service history with documented timing tensioner replacement (metal units)
* Post‑2000 build with cast‑iron liners (Nikasil issue resolved)
* Water pump replaced with the metal factory unit — this is a key preventative item
* No history of overheating — the aluminium block is unforgiving of heat
avoidIf: * Pre‑2000 engine with no documented Nikasil work — get a leakdown test
* Cold‑start rattle with no timing tensioner history — this is the single most important check
* Overheating history — do not buy an XJ that's been through an overheat
* Sludge on the valve covers — indicates previous overheating

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The XJ 4.0 V8 is a glorious engine — smooth, powerful and characterful. But it has two infamous weaknesses: Nikasil liners on pre‑2000 cars and plastic timing chain tensioners that can destroy the engine. Both are well understood and fixable, but you need to buy on documented history. If the tensioners have been replaced with metal units and the cooling system is well maintained, this is one of the most rewarding engines Jaguar ever built.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar XJ 4.0 V8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling/tapping noise (timing chain tensioner warning — the most critical check)
* Service History — Including which engine era (AJ26 pre‑2000 with Nikasil risk, AJ27 post‑2000 with iron liners) — see the Era Map above
* Timing Tensioners — Have the plastic tensioners been replaced with metal units? This is the number one preventative item
* Nikasil Check — For pre‑2000 cars, check engine number and consider a leakdown test
* Water Pump — Has the plastic water pump been replaced with the metal factory unit?
* Overheating History — Any record of overheating? The aluminium block is sensitive to heat
* Valve Cover Sludge — Check for sludge — indicates previous overheating
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, cooling, or misfire issues?
